findings.blocker: Three independent routes to a 'genuinely bivariate' morphism ℙ¹×𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ each hit a Mathlib infrastructure gap. The headline body is now 5-iter-deferred and the iter-169 plan's armed escalation trigger has fired.

attempts.strategy: Direct Proj.fromOfGlobalSections from the whole pullback (single global ring map, no chart-glue).
attempts.code_tried: Build φ : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ((ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm).left, ⊤) sending X 0 ↦ t, X 1 ↦ 1 and apply Proj.fromOfGlobalSections.
attempts.lean_error: Not a Lean-error per se — semantic dead-end. Γ(ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m, ⊤) = k̄[t, t⁻¹] (Γ(ℙ¹, ⊤) = k̄ by properness; tensor with GmRing); any morphism produced via this route factors through Spec(GmRing) = 𝔾_m, giving (x, λ) ↦ [λ : 1] = λ — the EMBEDDING 𝔾_m ↪ ℙ¹, NOT the scaling action (x, λ) ↦ λ·x.
attempts.goal_before: ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar (over Spec (.of kbar)).
attempts.goal_after: Unchanged — route abandoned semantically before Lean-error.
attempts.insight: The single-fromOfGlobalSections route from a non-affine source cannot encode a non-trivially-bivariate morphism. Global sections of a non-affine source LOSE the ℙ¹ factor.

attempts.strategy: Chart-glue (Option B per iter-169 plan): two chart-side ring maps φ_i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X i)) (GmRing kbar), Proj.fromOfGlobalSections per chart, glue via Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms over pullback cover.
attempts.code_tried: Scaffold construction of φ_i for i ∈ Fin 2 sending the chart-i unit-coord X i ↦ (unit lift) ⊗ 1 and the other coord X (otherFin i) ↦ (affine coord) ⊗ λ^±1; cocycle on D₊(X 0 · X 1).
attempts.lean_error: Did not reach Lean — bailout on LOC budget. ~200 LOC of TensorProduct + HomogeneousLocalization.Away ring-instance plumbing with no in-tree precedent + ~50 LOC cocycle. Specific blockers: (a) `TensorProduct kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 _) (GmRing kbar)` has no `CommRing`/`Algebra` instance shipped by Mathlib under `inferInstance`; (b) cocycle on `D₊(X 0 · X 1)` needs `Proj.basicOpen`-cover-restriction lemmas not specialised to `affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`.
attempts.insight: Option B is not closeable within an iter budget. The plan's '<200 LOC' estimate was optimistic. The decisive Mathlib gap is the missing CommRing/Algebra instance on `TensorProduct (HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ _) _` — Mathlib does not expose this canonical product ring as `inferInstance`-resolvable.

attempts.strategy: Functoriality-of-Proj route (NEW, not in the iter-169 plan): use `Proj.map : (𝒜 →+*ᵍ ℬ) → (Proj ℬ ⟶ Proj 𝒜)` from `ProjectiveSpectrum/Functor.lean:144` with 𝒜 = MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar, ℬ = MvPolynomial (Fin 2) (GmRing kbar), and the graded ring map X 0 ↦ t · X 0, X 1 ↦ X 1.
attempts.code_tried: Identify `Proj ℬ ≅ ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm` via a base-change iso (relative Proj). The morphism would then be Proj.map (φ) composed with that iso.
attempts.lean_error: Mathlib gap — `Proj.iso_pullback_Spec` / `ProjPolynomial.base_change_iso` / `relativeProj` declarations all VERIFIED ABSENT (via Glob + Grep across the entire Mathlib `AlgebraicGeometry/` tree). The relative-Proj base-change iso `Proj(MvPoly (Fin n) R) ≅ Proj(MvPoly (Fin n) k) ×_{Spec k} Spec R` does not exist.
attempts.insight: Route is mathematically the CLEANEST but gated on a missing upstream Mathlib infrastructure (relative-Proj base-change). Would be a multi-iter Mathlib PR (~5-iter detour). Reusable evidence that the relative-Proj formalism is the real bottleneck here — recording it for the iter-170 user escalation.
